info.name: XDB API Set - Developers
info.description: The XDB Memory Management API Suite provides a robust set of endpoints for efficiently managing and monitoring memory resources in your application or system. Designed for developers and system administrators, these APIs enable precise control over memory allocation, utilization tracking, and cleanup processes, ensuring optimal performance and stability.

request.description:
# Activate User Account

This endpoint is used to activate a user account.

## Request Body

- `name`: The name of the user to be activated.
    

## Response

- `status`: The status of the activation process.
    
- `message`: A message related to the activation process.
    
- `data`: An object containing the apiKey and status of the activation.
    
- `timestamp`: The timestamp of the activation process.
    
- `processId`: The ID of the activation process.

response.name: success-response

request.description:
### Create Profile

This endpoint allows the user to create a profile with the provided name, identification, and identification type.

#### Request Body

- name: (string) The name of the user.
    
- identification: (string) The identification of the user.
    
- identificationType: (string) The type of identification.
    

#### Response

The response will be a JSON object with the following properties:

- status: (string) The status of the response.
    
- message: (string) Any additional message related to the response.
    
- data: (object) An object containing the userkey, name, identification, and profileId.
    
    - userkey: (string) The user key.
        
    - name: (string) The name of the user.
        
    - identification: (string) The identification of the user.
        
    - profileId: (number) The ID of the created profile.
        
- timestamp: (string) The timestamp of the response.
    
- processId: (string) The process ID associated with the response.
    

#### JSON Schema

``` json
{
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "status": { "type": "string" },
    "message": { "type": "string" },
    "data": {
      "type": "object",
      "properties": {
        "userkey": { "type": "string" },
        "name": { "type": "string" },
        "identification": { "type": "string" },
        "profileId": { "type": "number" }
      }
    },
    "timestamp": { "type": "string" },
    "processId": { "type": "string" }
  }
}

 ```

item.description: This covers the complete suite of API requests for user management tasks. It includes creating new users, updating user details, retrieving user profiles, and deleting accounts. Use these requests to validate and test your user management endpoints, ensuring robust access control and streamlined onboarding/offboarding processes.

request.description:
### Create Memory

This endpoint allows you to create a new memory by submitting the required data via the request body.

#### Request Body

- `userKey`: The user key associated with the memory.
    
- `content`: The content of the memory.
    
- `tag`: The tag associated with the memory.
    
- `sessionId`: The session ID for the memory.
    

Example:

``` json
{
  "userKey": "fd4631e3b92d3f3ed5ad8f970269bd ...",
  "content": "Suchitra Padmanabhan is the pr ...",
  "tag": "",
  "sessionId": "2025032602"
}
#### Response
- `status`: The status of the response.
- `message`: Additional information or error message.
- `data`: The data object containing the created memory.
- `timestamp`: The timestamp of the response.
- `processId`: The ID of the process.
Example:
```json
{
  "status": "",
  "message": "",
  "data": {},
  "timestamp": "",
  "processId": ""
}

 ```

request.description:
This endpoint allows you to retrieve a list of tags associated with memory notes. The HTTP POST request should be sent to {{base_url}}/api/memory/list-tag.

### Request Body

- userKey (string, required): The user key for authentication.
    

### Response

- status (string): The status of the response.
    
- message (string): A message related to the response status.
    
- data (object): Contains the list of notes tags.
    
    - notesTag (array): An array of tags associated with memory notes.
        
- timestamp (string): The timestamp of the response.
    
- processId (string): The process ID associated with the response.

request.description:
This endpoint allows you to add a tag to a user's memory. The request should be sent as an HTTP POST to {{base_url}}/api/memory/add-tag. The request body should be in raw format and include the following parameters:

- userKey: (string) The key identifying the user.
    
- sessionId: (string) The session ID for the memory.
    
- tag: (string) The tag to be added to the memory.
    

The response will include the updated memory details after adding the tag.

request.description:
### POST /api/memory/by-tag

This endpoint is used to retrieve memories by tag for a specific user.

#### Request Body

- userKey (string): The unique key of the user.
    
- tag (string): The tag for which memories are to be retrieved.
    

#### Response

The response will be a JSON object with the following properties:

- status (string): The status of the response.
    
- message (string): Any additional message related to the response.
    
- data (object): The data object containing an array of memories.
    
    - memories (array): An array of memory objects.
        
        - memory (string): The memory content.
            
        - language (string): The language of the memory.
            
        - transactionNumber (string): The transaction number associated with the memory.
            
        - date (string): The date of the memory.
            
        - docId (string): The document ID of the memory.
            
        - fileID (string): The file ID of the memory.
            
        - notesKey (string): The key for the notes related to the memory.
            
        - tokens (array): An array of tokens associated with the memory.
            
- timestamp (string): The timestamp of the response.
    
- processId (string): The process ID associated with the response.
    

#### Example

``` json
{
  "status": "",
  "message": "",
  "data": {
    "memories": [
      {
        "memory": "",
        "language": null,
        "transactionNumber": null,
        "date": "",
        "docId": null,
        "fileID": null,
        "notesKey": "",
        "tokens": [""]
      }
    ]
  },
  "timestamp": "",
  "processId": ""
}

 ```

request.description:
### Change Memory Tag

This endpoint allows the user to update the tag associated with a specific memory.

#### Request Body

- `userKey` (string): The unique key of the user.
    
- `currentTag` (string): The current tag associated with the memory.
    
- `updatedTag` (string): The new tag to be associated with the memory.
    

#### Response

- `status` (string): The status of the response.
    
- `message` (string): Additional information or message related to the response.
    
- `data`
    
    - `profileId` (number): The profile ID associated with the memory.
        
    - `status` (string): The status of the operation.
        
    - `updatedCount` (number): The count of memories updated.
        
- `timestamp` (string): The timestamp of the response.
    
- `processId` (string): The process ID associated with the response.
